LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT MEGAN ACKERLEY'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION IS GRANTED IN PART. THE LEVYING OFFICER IS INSTRUCTED TO RETURN ALL AMOUNTS OVER $4000.00 TO THE CLAIMANT/JUDGMENT DEBTOR AND TO RELEASE $4000.00 TO THE JUDGMENT CREDITOR AS PAYMENT FOR THE JUDGMENT. calendar.matters:
HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
Off Calendar May-12-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
HEARING OF DEFENDANT MEGAN ACKERLEY'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is Off-calendar, Claim of Exemption was withdrawn by Defendant per San Francisco Sheriff Department. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter; Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976

Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption And Ntc Of Hearing
Off Calendar Aug-20-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Robert F. Cohen, Esq. of the Law Office of Robert Cohen, Tel. 800.492.2657, specially appearing for plaintiff and moving party;
Trevor Caudle, Esq. of Trevor Caudle Law, PC, Tel. 415.260.0947, for defendant/claimant Megan Ackerley who was present in court with Charles Ackerley.

RULING: Plaintiff's counsel represented to the Court that Wells Fargo has no interest in the proceeds of this case. The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court ruled as follows: DEFENDANT MEGAN ACKERLEY'S CLAIM OF EXEMPTION is granted in part. The levying officer is instructed to return all amounts over $4000.00 to the claimant/judgment debtor and to release $4000.00 to the judgment creditor as payment for the judgment. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. (Clerk shall serve the order on all parties.) Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR# 5976

Ntc Of Hearing On Claim Of Exemption And Ntc Of Hearing
Off Calendar Jul-30-2014 Continued To Aug-20-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
